title: AI Talent Index — U.S. vs China
subtitle: Three-proxy composite: research volume, quality papers (cited ≥10), and high-impact output (cited ≥100). Each proxy scored as share-of-combined (US + China = 100). US + China composite ≈ 100 by construction.
description: A composite index measuring relative AI talent strength across three dimensions: (1) AI paper volume (breadth of research output), (2) quality AI papers with ≥10 citations in 2 years (research impact), and (3) high-impact AI papers with ≥100 citations in 3 years (elite research production). All proxies sourced from OpenAlex. Each is scored as US share of combined US+China total.

paper_volume.note: AI papers (AI/ML/NLP/CV concepts) on OpenAlex, last 365 days, grouped by author institution country. China leads on volume; US tends to lead on citation impact.

top_conference.note: AI papers with at least 25 citations, last 730 days. No venue filter — OpenAlex classifies most AI papers as arXiv preprints regardless of conference venue, making source.type:conference unreliable. Citation threshold is a more objective and reproducible quality proxy.

high_impact.note: AI papers of any type with at least 100 citations, last 1095 days. Uses a 3-year window to give papers adequate time to accumulate citations. Captures field-defining research regardless of venue.

paper_volume.note: China leads on AI paper volume (231,714 vs US 130,881 over 365 days). OpenAlex may undercount Chinese domestic journals not indexed in major international databases.

top_conference.note: China has substantially increased high-quality AI publication output since 2019. Citation thresholding filters out the long tail of low-engagement papers, isolating papers with measurable research impact.

high_impact.note: US tends to lead on high-citation AI papers, reflecting concentration of frontier lab research (Google DeepMind, OpenAI, Meta FAIR, Microsoft Research) in the US ecosystem.

interpretive_sentence: On a composite of paper volume (30%), quality papers cited ≥25x (40%), and high-impact output cited ≥100x (30%), the US scores 37.1 and China scores 62.9 out of 100 (US + China ≈ 100). China leads on raw paper volume (63.9% of combined); the US leads on quality cited papers (32.3%) and high-impact papers (44.6%).

composite_construction.method: Weighted average of three share-of-combined scores. Each proxy is computed as US/(US+China)*100, giving a score where US+China=100. Composite = 0.30*(paper_volume_share) + 0.40*(quality_papers_share) + 0.30*(high_impact_share). Quality papers = cited ≥25x in 2 years; high-impact = cited ≥100x in 3 years. No venue filter — citation thresholds are a more robust quality proxy than OpenAlex venue-type classification, which is unreliable for AI papers.

source.name: OpenAlex API
source.note: group_by=authorships.institutions.country_code on AI/ML/NLP/CV concepts. Papers with authors from multiple countries are counted in each country. Quality proxy uses citation threshold only — no venue-type filter.

papers.title: ChatGPT as a Tool for Programming Assistance and Code Development
The Vinness Wagyu Theorem: On the Epistemic Independence of Direct Knowledge from Formal Verification
VGExplainer: Linking Graph Neural Networks and Large Language Models for Explainable Vulnerability Detection
A review of deep learning methods for non-invasive fetal electrocardiography
Artificial neural network-based non-parametric ground motion models for multiple intensity measures in Türkiye
Cellular and subcellular specialization enables biology-constrained deep learning
PCD-GAN: A perceptual and copyright dual generative adversarial network for artwork style transfer and copyright protection
Mapping fusion zones to motion resistance: Finite element insights into return-to-activity after interbody fusion
SOS1: tracking the evolving path from promising to actionable therapeutic target in RAS-dependent cancers
Longitudinal visualization tools for advanced characterization of multiple sclerosis lesions using diffusion MRI and magnetization transfer imaging metrics
A Formal Framework for Evaluating Reasoning Integrity in Language Models
Applications of artificial intelligence in peripheral neurosurgery: a systematic review
Physiological Expression Mapping in Cardiometabolic Disease: A State-Space Framework with Clinical and Hemodynamic Evidence

methodology_note: All three proxies use OpenAlex's group_by endpoint. Country attribution follows OpenAlex's institution-to-country mapping (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2). A paper with US and Chinese co-authors is counted in both country totals. The quality proxy uses only a citation threshold (no venue filter) because OpenAlex classifies most AI papers as arXiv preprints regardless of the conference where they appeared, making source.type:conference unreliable. Known limitations: OpenAlex undercounts Chinese domestic journals/conferences not indexed internationally; citation counts favor English-language venues.
